slice.name: account-registration-request
slice.scope: Public account registration request, Team Admin review queue, GOV.UK form behaviour, preview/prod API delivery and operating-model learnings

purpose: Record the branch-level agent activity for the account registration request work, replacing an overly narrow CI-fix trace with an audit of the full implementation and correction path.

initial_user_request.summary: Build registration, not login, so users can request an account without receiving a role, and give Team Admins enough information to review the request.
constraints: No role is applied during registration.
Requested role or work type is review information only.
Do not expose programmatic wording in errors.
Start a branch and do not open a PR until asked.

dominant_rules: Keep account registration separate from role assignment.
Use GOV.UK-style content, validation and interaction patterns.
Make branch work end to end in preview and production, not only as static page rendering.
Capture durable lessons in RECENT_LEARNINGS.md and relevant bundles when the review reveals reusable mistakes.

decision_path.interpretation: Registration is an access request, not a role assignment transaction.
changes: Created public account request page.
Created Team Admin read-only review page.
Added auth registration request route.
Added D1 review queue table.
Linked sign-in page to registration.
Added route-state test to assert no role assignment during registration.

decision_path.interpretation: The model was right, but the journey still sounded too role-selection-led and needed stronger GOV.UK pattern fidelity.
changes: Changed the main question to what the user needs to use ResearchOps for.
Changed radio labels to activity-based labels.
Removed the public-page notification banner.
Added check-answers step.
Changed duplicate pending request handling to HTTP 200 with created:false.
Added runtime tests using a fake D1 store.

decision_path.interpretation: Full-width fields and cramped intro-to-form rhythm were GOV.UK form-affordance issues that should persist as bundle learning.
changes: Added public/css/auth-registration.css.
Applied two-thirds widths to common fields and textarea.
Added vertical-rhythm spacing between intro content and the form.
Added govuk-form-affordance-reference.xml.
Updated GOV.UK bundle metadata and changelog.
Updated RECENT_LEARNINGS.md and .agent-operating-model/README.md.

decision_path.interpretation: The branch preview rendered the page but did not guarantee the API route existed or accepted the preview origin.
changes: Changed registration frontend to try relative /api first.
Added preview and production Worker fallbacks.
Applied the same API routing model to the Team Admin review page.
Updated Worker CORS to allow researchops.pages.dev and *.researchops.pages.dev.
Added preview Worker deployment for feature branches.
Renumbered registration migration from 0004 to 0005 to avoid conflict with main.
Updated tests to assert preview-safe API routing and migration deployment.

decision_path.interpretation: Change links must reveal the relevant answer field, and passive containers should not receive yellow focus rings.
changes: Removed tabindex from the check-answers section.
Changed transition to scroll the section into view instead of focusing the container.
Added data-change-target to each Change link.
Added a click handler that reveals the form and focuses the relevant field.
Added route-state assertions for change-link behaviour and absence of passive-container focus.

decision_path.interpretation: A trace that only described the CI trace-coverage fix was too narrow and did not satisfy the purpose of agent audit traces.
changes: Deleted account-registration-request-pr-fix-trace.md.
Deleted account-registration-request-pr-fix-trace.json.
Created account-registration-request-branch-trace.md.
Created account-registration-request-branch-trace.json.

validation_added: Route-state assertions for GOV.UK content and form affordance.
Route-state assertions for preview-safe API routing and branch-preview CORS.
Route-state assertions for preview Worker deployment and migration path.
Route-state assertions for check-answers change-link behaviour.
Runtime tests for valid request, duplicate request and invalid email handling.
Runtime assertion that the registration route does not prepare SQL touching auth_role_assignments.
known_limitations: Role assignment is not yet linked back to request resolution.
No rate limiting or abuse controls yet.
Team or service remains free text.
Visual walkthrough registry and report regeneration remain outstanding.
trace_boundary: This file records task interpretation, decisions, implementation stages, pivots and validation evidence. It does not expose private chain-of-thought.
